title: Fix `--hierarchical` finding include files
body:
The `--hierarchical` option does not look for sources using `-I` like non-hierarchical mode when sources are not in the current directory. 

This happens on master running linux. 

Test case contains 2 sources that are 2-level deep in hierarchy and I tried 2 verilator invocations. One works and one doesn't.
```
Apptainer> more */*/*
::::::::::::::
build/src/mid.v
::::::::::::::
module mid(input clock, input reset, input a, input b, output c); /* verilator hier_block */

assign c = a & b;
endmodule
::::::::::::::
build/src/top.v
::::::::::::::
module top(input clock, input reset);

wire a,b,c;

mid mid(clock, reset, a, b, c);


endmodule
Apptainer> ../build/src/verilator -Ibuild/src  top.v mid.v --top-module top --cc 
- V e r i l a t i o n   R e p o r t:  rev v5.030 (mod)
- Verilator: Built from 0.033 MB sources in 3 modules, into 0.018 MB in 6 C++ files needing 0.000 MB
- Verilator: Walltime 0.025 s (elab=0.001, cvt=0.008, bld=0.000); cpu 0.025 s on 1 threads; alloced 9.930 MB
Apptainer> rm -rf mdir ; ../build/src/verilator -Ibuild/src  top.v mid.v --top-module top --cc  --hierarchical
make: Entering directory '/home/awmoore/pta/verilator/test/obj_dir'
make: *** No rule to make target 'top.v', needed by 'Vtop.mk'.  Stop.
make: Leaving directory '/home/awmoore/pta/verilator/test/obj_dir'
%Error: make -C obj_dir -f Vtop_hier.mk  -j 1  hier_verilation exited with 2
Apptainer> 
```
